rationale: A visible and rapid concentration of armored units and airpower on the Tethys border will deter aggression and signal resolve without triggering open conflict.
Disabling Tethys’ logistics coordination systems would degrade their mobilization capacity and buy time for our conventional forces to consolidate.
Conducting a high-profile joint-service exercise near the border reinforces domestic morale and demonstrates operational readiness to both allies and adversaries.
Orchestrating a plausible false flag incident can delegitimize Tethys’ domestic narrative of victimhood and fracture international support for their position.
Infiltrating and exfiltrating sensitive defense procurement data will expose vulnerabilities and inform future asymmetric targeting.
Discreetly compromising key Tethys military decision-makers through kompromat establishes leverage for future coercive diplomacy.
Locking in critical reserves of energy and industrial inputs now insulates our war economy from Tethys’ potential pipeline disruptions.
Issuing a targeted war bond campaign channels the current metals windfall into state-controlled war financing while managing inflationary pressures.
Preemptively restricting rare earth exports to Novaris undermines their advanced weapons production before hostilities escalate.
Demonstrates readiness and deters Novaris by showcasing rapid mobilization capacity along the border.
Maintains constant pressure and surveillance on Novaris’ movements without triggering direct escalation.
Disrupts Novaris military logistics systems asymmetrically, exploiting their overextension.
Targets critical energy infrastructure nodes in Novaris to amplify economic strain from falling export revenues.
Creates plausible deniability for internal instability within Novaris by framing dissident factions.
Steals defense planning data to anticipate Novaris’ next moves and inform asymmetric counter-strategies.
Isolates Novaris diplomatically by forming a united front of energy-importing states concerned about supply security.
Opens discreet de-escalation pathways to avoid miscalculation while maintaining public toughness.
Projects soft power and gains international goodwill by positioning us as stabilizers amid crisis.
Locks in critical metals at peak prices to insulate domestic industry from future volatility.
Weaponizes our export advantage by restricting metals flow to Novaris’ defense sector.
Weakens our currency to boost non-metals exports and diversify economic resilience beyond commodity dependence.
